senses.definition: to briefly live in one or several places, without ever unpacking one's luggage, often due to not having a permanent place to live or stay in

examples.x: Sarah's job involves so much travelling that she lives out of a suitcase.
As a travel journalist, she lives out of a suitcase, exploring new destinations every week.
His job requires him to live out of a suitcase, as he travels to different cities for business meetings.
The musician's busy touring schedule means he has to live out of a suitcase for most of the year.

senses.definition: a strong urge to travel or leave somewhere

examples.x: Ever since he was a child, he had itchy feet and dreamt of exploring distant lands.
After retiring, she couldn't ignore her itchy feet and decided to fulfill her travel aspirations.
His constant itchy feet led him to change jobs frequently, seeking new challenges and experiences.
She felt the pull of itchy feet and took a year off to travel and see the world.
Even though she had a comfortable home, her itchy feet drove her to go on a backpacking adventure across Europe.

senses.definition: in an active state of travel by car, bus, or other form of transportation

examples.x: She's often on the road, visiting clients and promoting her business.
The traveling circus is constantly on the road, moving from one town to another.
During the summer vacation, families across the country are on the road, heading to their favorite holiday destinations.
As a long-haul trucker, he spends most of his time on the road, driving goods across the country.
Retiring early, they decided to be on the road, exploring different countries and cultures in their motorhome.

senses.definition: the instinctive desire to return to a more primitive, untamed way of life, often associated with the wilderness or natural world

examples.x: Growing up so close to the forest, the child found it hard to resist the call of the wild, preferring the company of the trees to that of her peers.
Longing for adventure, she felt the call of the wild and decided to go on a hiking expedition deep into the wilderness.
The call of the wild is a powerful force that can lead people to leave behind their urban lives and embrace a simpler existence in nature.
The wanderer couldn't resist the call of the wild, leaving behind the comforts of home to explore the remote forests and mountains.
In literature and film, characters often heed the call of the wild, embarking on journeys that lead them to discover their true selves in the wilderness.

senses.definition: to leave a location, usually to embark on a journey or trip

examples.x: After packing the car, it was time to hit the road and start our cross-country adventure.
We had a great weekend getaway, but now it's time to hit the road and head back home.
As the sun rose, the group of friends gathered their backpacks and decided to hit the road for a day of exploration.
The touring band was ready to hit the road, bringing their music to different cities and venues.
With a sense of anticipation, they fueled up the RV and prepared to hit the road for their summer vacation.

senses.definition: to examine the chances of something's success before trying it

examples.x: Before making a career change, she decided to test the waters by taking on a part-time job in the new field.
They decided to test the waters by launching a small pilot project to gauge public interest before investing in a larger venture.
Rather than committing to a long-term relationship right away, they agreed to test the waters and go on a few casual dates first.
The company decided to test the waters in international markets with a limited product release to assess demand and response.
In negotiations, it's often wise to test the waters by proposing initial terms to see how the other party responds before finalizing an agreement.
